title: Booking a Yoga Retreat
summary: Lia calls a retreat centre to ask about a five-day yoga retreat by the coast — accommodation, meals, level, and how to confirm her booking.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Hello, I'm calling about the five-day yoga retreat next month. Do you still have places?

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: Let me check. Yes, we have three places left. Have you been to one of our retreats before?

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: No, it's my first time. I've been doing yoga for about a year, but only at the studio near my house.

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: That's fine. The retreat is suitable for all levels. We have two classes a day, one gentle in the morning and a stronger one in the late afternoon.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: What kind of accommodation do you offer?

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: We have shared rooms for two and a few single rooms. The single rooms cost a little more, but they are quieter.

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: Yes, three vegetarian meals a day, all made with local vegetables. Tea and fruit are available between classes.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: That sounds wonderful. How far is the retreat from the train station?

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: About twenty minutes by car. We can organise a shared shuttle from the station if you tell us your arrival time.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Perfect. How do I confirm my booking?

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: Pay a deposit of one hundred pounds within the next seven days. The rest is due one week before the retreat starts.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: And if I have to cancel?

body.speaker: Operator
body.text: You can cancel for a full refund up to two weeks before the retreat. After that, we keep the deposit.

annotations.exampleEn: She booked a weekend yoga retreat in the mountains.
Are there any places left for tomorrow?
The course is open to all levels.
She took a gentle walk after dinner.
Shared rooms are cheaper than single ones.
All vegetarian meals are made with local vegetables.
A shared shuttle picks guests up from the airport.

annotations.meaningVi: xác nhận đặt chỗ của tôi
annotations.exampleEn: How can I confirm my booking online?
Please pay a deposit to hold the place.
You can ask for a full refund within fourteen days.

annotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi giữ lại tiền đặt cọc
annotations.exampleEn: If you cancel late, we keep the deposit.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect continuous for ongoing activity
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng have/has + been + V-ing để nói một hoạt động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và đang tiếp diễn. Trong bài: 'I've been doing yoga for about a year.' (bắt đầu cách đây một năm và vẫn còn đang tập).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'have/has + been + V-ing' for an activity that started in the past and is still going on. From the dialogue: 'I've been doing yoga for about a year' — she started a year ago and still practises.

grammarNotes.title: First conditional for booking conditions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1: If + hiện tại đơn, will + V. Dùng để nói về kết quả nếu điều gì đó xảy ra. Trong bài: 'You can cancel for a full refund up to two weeks before the retreat. After that, we keep the deposit.' (ngầm chứa điều kiện 'nếu cô huỷ sau đó').
grammarNotes.bodyEn: First conditional uses 'If + present simple, will/can + verb' to describe a likely outcome. From the dialogue, the cancellation policy is essentially a conditional: 'If you cancel after two weeks, we keep the deposit.'

translationVi:
Lia: Xin chào, tôi gọi để hỏi về khoá yoga 5 ngày tháng tới. Bên mình còn chỗ không?
Operator: Để tôi kiểm tra. Có, còn ba suất. Cô đã từng tham gia khoá nào của chúng tôi chưa?
Lia: Chưa, đây là lần đầu. Tôi mới tập yoga khoảng một năm, chỉ ở phòng tập gần nhà.
Operator: Không sao. Khoá này phù hợp với mọi trình độ. Mỗi ngày có hai lớp, một lớp nhẹ buổi sáng và một lớp nặng hơn vào cuối chiều.
Lia: Chỗ ở thì sao?
Operator: Bên tôi có phòng đôi dùng chung và một vài phòng đơn. Phòng đơn đắt hơn một chút, nhưng yên tĩnh hơn.
Lia: Có bao gồm bữa ăn không?
Operator: Có, ba bữa chay mỗi ngày, đều làm từ rau địa phương. Giữa các lớp có sẵn trà và trái cây.
Lia: Nghe tuyệt quá. Khu nghỉ cách ga tàu bao xa?
Operator: Khoảng hai mươi phút lái xe. Chúng tôi có thể sắp xe đưa đón chung từ ga nếu cô báo giờ tới.
Lia: Hoàn hảo. Tôi xác nhận đặt chỗ thế nào ạ?
Operator: Đặt cọc một trăm bảng trong vòng bảy ngày tới. Phần còn lại đóng trước khoá học một tuần.
Lia: Và nếu tôi phải huỷ?
Operator: Cô có thể huỷ và nhận lại toàn bộ tiền nếu báo trước hai tuần. Sau thời gian đó, chúng tôi sẽ giữ lại tiền cọc.

options: To ask about a five-day yoga retreat next month
questions.explanation: 'I'm calling about the five-day yoga retreat next month.'

questions.prompt: How many places are still available?
questions.hint: Listen for a small number after she asks about availability.

questions.prompt: How long has Lia been doing yoga?
questions.explanation: 'I've been doing yoga for about a year.'
questions.hint: Listen for a length of time.

questions.prompt: How many classes are held each day?
questions.hint: Listen for the number of daily classes.

questions.prompt: Which class is gentler — morning or afternoon?
questions.explanation: 'one gentle in the morning and a stronger one in the late afternoon.'
questions.hint: Listen for the contrast between the two classes.

questions.prompt: Why might someone choose a single room?
options: Single rooms are quieter, though they cost a little more
Single rooms are larger and brighter
questions.explanation: 'The single rooms cost a little more, but they are quieter.'
questions.hint: Listen for the trade-off between cost and quiet.

options: Three vegetarian meals a day with local vegetables
questions.explanation: 'three vegetarian meals a day, all made with local vegetables.'
questions.hint: Listen for the number of meals and their type.

questions.prompt: How far is the retreat from the train station?
How can guests get from the station to the retreat?
options: Use a shared shuttle organised by the retreat
questions.explanation: 'We can organise a shared shuttle from the station.'
questions.hint: Listen for what the centre can arrange.

questions.prompt: What deposit is needed to confirm the booking?
questions.explanation: 'Pay a deposit of one hundred pounds within the next seven days.'

options: Full refund up to two weeks before; after that the deposit is kept
Refund only if the guest is ill
questions.explanation: 'You can cancel for a full refund up to two weeks before the retreat. After that, we keep the deposit.'
questions.hint: Listen for the two parts of the policy.

cloze.template:
Lia: Hello, I'm calling about the five-day yoga {{b1}} next month. Do you still have places?
Operator: Let me check. Yes, we have three places {{b2}}. Have you been to one of our retreats before?
Lia: No, it's my first time. I've been doing yoga for about a year, but only at the studio near my house.
Operator: That's fine. The retreat is suitable for all {{b3}}. We have two classes a day, one gentle in the morning and a {{b4}} one in the late afternoon.
Lia: What kind of accommodation do you offer?
Operator: We have {{b5}} rooms for two and a few single rooms. The single rooms cost a little more, but they are quieter.
Lia: Are meals {{b6}}?
Operator: Yes, three vegetarian meals a day, all made with local vegetables. Tea and fruit are available between classes.
Lia: That sounds wonderful. How far is the retreat from the train station?
Operator: About twenty minutes by car. We can organise a shared {{b7}} from the station if you tell us your arrival time.
Lia: Perfect. How do I {{b8}} my booking?
Operator: Pay a {{b9}} of one hundred pounds within the next seven days. The rest is due one week before the retreat starts.
Lia: And if I have to cancel?
Operator: You can cancel for a full refund up to two {{b10}} before the retreat. After that, we keep the deposit.

blanks.explanation: 'retreat' — a multi-day quiet stay focused on yoga.
'stronger' — the opposite of 'gentle' in this contrast.
'shuttle' — a short transport between two fixed points.
'deposit' — money paid in advance to secure the booking.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Lia is paying to spend five quiet days doing yoga and eating simple food. Why do you think some people need to leave home to feel rested, while others find rest by staying where they are? Which kind of rest works better for you?
